mnemonics: S.U.P.E.R. (Super Understands Parent's Explicit Resources) - Use `super` to access parent class members explicitly.
keyPoints: `super` is used to call the superclass constructor from the subclass constructor.
`super` can be used to access methods of the superclass that have been overridden in the subclass.
`super` is used to access hidden fields of the superclass if the subclass has a field with the same name.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call `super()` as the first statement in a subclass constructor when the superclass doesn't have a default (no-arg) constructor.
Using `super` to access private members of the superclass, which is not allowed due to encapsulation.
